assessments.status_rationale: Today’s bullets focus on the Iran conflict and politics but do not report an EU Council Decision/Implementing Regulation adopting a new Iran-related restrictive-measures package or adding ≥20 new listings.

No item in today’s bullets indicates the U.S. Department of Energy announced an SPR release (sale or exchange) totaling ≥10 million barrels, nor that it explicitly cited Middle East escalation as a primary driver.
The news list contains no reported detention/seizure/diversion by Iran of a non-Iranian-flagged commercial oil/product tanker in/near the Strait of Hormuz lasting ≥24 hours.

While there are travel-related headlines about the Iran conflict’s impacts, none state that DXB or DOH suspended commercial passenger departures/arrivals for ≥12 consecutive hours due to security risk.
Today’s bullets include market commentary about rising fuel-price risk but provide no evidence that AAA’s national average retail gasoline price exceeded $4.25/gal on any day.

The evaluation window runs from the 2026-03-03 close to the 2026-06-30 close; today’s headlines discuss market volatility but do not (and cannot yet) establish the required XLE vs. SPY outperformance over the full period.
No bullet indicates Amazon disclosed charges and/or expected costs of ≥$250M tied to UAE/Bahrain facility damage from reported drone strikes.

A MarketWatch item mentions Nvidia generally, but there is no clear reporting here of Nvidia publicly launching/announcing a data-center networking product/platform explicitly described by Nvidia as using “silicon photonics” or “co-packaged optics.”
No bullet references CDC official surveillance totals for U.S. measles cases, and nothing indicates the 2026 YTD cumulative reached ≥1,000.
Today’s bullets do not mention New York State enacting (governor signing) a new congressional district map to replace the prior map for the 2026 U.S. House elections.
